steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Two independent clauses are joined by the conjunctive adverb "though." Before the blank: "The sand at Balos Beach in Greece is a more unusual shade" — a complete independent clause. After the blank: "deposits of crushed coral and other organic matter lend the sand a unique pink hue" — another complete independent clause. The punctuation must correctly join them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — shade, though: Two independent clauses joined only by a comma is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — shade: though: Colons don't introduce conjunctive adverbs. ✗
  • Choice C — shade, though,: Treating "though" as a parenthetical breaks the logical flow between the two related clauses. ✗
  • Choice D — shade, though;: The correct pattern for a conjunctive adverb joining two independent clauses: IC, though; IC. ✓
That matches Choice D — shade, though;.
